title: What Considered
objective: Memorize the shown pulse sequence, then replay only the beats that pass the current Considered rule while building combo before three mistakes end the run.

controls.input: Hold the phone normally and observe the pads pulsing in sequence
controls.outcome: You learn the full pattern and hear each pad's musical cue
Inputs that match the valid next beat score and extend combo

controls.input: Do not tap when the next memorized beat is currently disallowed by the rule
controls.outcome: The sequence advances safely and preserves your run

controls.input: Tap the glowing Considered badge during its brief flash
controls.outcome: You reveal the next rule change early, helping plan the rest of the replay

steps: A short sequence of 3-4 pads is performed with bold audio pulses and screen shake.
The Considered badge declares a live filter such as 'replay only cool colors,' 'replay only outer ring beats,' or 'replay only sounds above
During replay, every few beats the Considered rule flips, forcing you to continue the remembered sequence but instantly decide which beats
Correct taps chain combo and fill a multiplier meter; wrong taps or tapping a rejected beat costs a life.
Each round adds one beat, speeds the tempo slightly, and introduces tighter rule-flip timing until the 2-4 minute run collapses.
scoring: Score 10 points per correct beat, plus combo-based multipliers at x2, x3, and x5 every 6 clean decisions. Perfectly handling a rule flip grants a 'Considered' bonus. Highest single-run score feeds a simple offline local/
tips: Memorize rhythm groups, not single pads.
When the badge is about to flip, stop chasing speed and prepare for the new filter.
Skipping correctly is as important as tapping correctly.
Use sound color and pad position together; late rounds are too fast for visual memory alone.

trendContext.title: What is considered too big for an indie project?
